page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8369B processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 4 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz and includes 48 MB of L3 cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or a GPU, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates average performance in single-core stress-ng tests and single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core performance is weak, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3875.1 and a Passmark CPU score of 5885.48. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with read-only large block speeds at 29.48 GB/sec and write speeds at 24.4 GB/sec, both falling into the lower performance tier. Conversely, cryptographic operations perform at an average level, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 3646.27, supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AVX-512 VNNI and AES.

From a resource density perspective, the ecs.g7.xlarge provides a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio typical of general-purpose workloads, offering dedicated CPU allocation without the cost overhead of memory-optimized or storage-optimized instances. The primary tradeoff is the lower multi-threaded efficiency and restricted memory bandwidth, which limits its utility for memory-bound or highly parallelized compute tasks. This instance is best suited for workloads such as lightweight web serving, single-threaded applications, basic database operations, and small-scale language model inference, where prompt processing and text generation for small models operate at average speeds.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8369B processor featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a 2.7 GHz clock speed, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Benchmark data reveals average single-core CPU performance and encryption capabilities, alongside weak multi-core execution and memory bandwidth. Qualitatively, the instance offers a balanced resource profile for general-purpose workloads without the premium of specialized hardware. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded applications, and small-scale database operations that do not require high multi-threaded throughput or intensive memory bandwidth.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.xlarge is a general-purpose KVM instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, showing average single-core and weak multi-core performance.

tagline: A dedicated general-purpose virtual server offering balanced compute and memory resources for single-threaded applications and lightweight workloads.

Average single-core CPU and cryptographic performance
Weak multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth
Baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps with no local storage
Suitable for lightweight web serving and small-scale database operations
